title: eval_exists_test needlessly collects into Vec<&Form>
description: eval_exists_test (evaluator.rs line 600) does let tmp: Vec<&Form> = forms.iter().collect() solely to pass to collect_one_string_list, which accepts &[&Form]. Unlike the other test functions, eval_exists_test does not need to filter through extract_match_type or extract_comparator first, so the indirection buys nothing. Either collect_one_string_list should be overloaded/genericised to accept &[Form] directly, or eval_exists_test should call collect_one_string_list with a forms.iter().collect() at the call site where the chaining genuinely requires it. As written, a Vec is allocated and immediately discarded just to satisfy a type mismatch.
